title: Travel Ban — Immigration News & Alerts — ELLIS PORTER
content:
The Biden Administration has allowed the temporary work visa ban (Presidential Proclamation 10052) to expire on March 31, 2021 and will not extend it.  While this is welcome news, individuals may still face challenges traveling to the U.S. due to other COVID-19 measures, including regional travel bans, consular service limitations and COVID-19 testing requirements.
In a 5-4 decision, the Supreme Court upheld the travel restrictions outlined in Travel Ban version 3.0.
Nearly a year has passed since the first travel ban was announced.  It has been a bumpy legal road.  This week, the Supreme Court sided with the government, allowing the ban to be implemented while legal wrangling continues.
A Ninth Circuit ruling has resumed the Travel Ban for those without U.S. ties.
The travel ban has been expanded and implemented indefinitely.  Get the details of what new countries have been added to the list.
Ellis Porter provides analysis as portions of the travel ban go into effect tonight.
President Donald Trump’s revised travel ban is set to take effect tomorrow, March 16, 2017.  This second Executive Order (EO) completely revokes and takes the place of the first order , which was widely derided and ultimately halted by the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals in early February.  The revised travel ban raises many new questions, which Ellis Porter answers in this helpful FAQ.
The Justice Department has filed an appeal with the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals seeking to overrule the nationwide temporary restraining order that has halted implementation of the travel ban Executive Order.
A temporary restraining order issued by a federal District Court judge in Washington state immediately halts further implementation of the travel ban Executive Order nationwide.
Today, President Trump signed his third immigration-related Executive Order (EO), entitled "Protecting the Nation from Foreign Terrorist Entry into the United States," which bans individuals from seven countries, as well as refugees, from entering the U.S.
